fact: For Kharif crops, land preparation should start after the first monsoon showers. Deep plowing every three years helps in soil aeration and pest control.
Jeevamrut is a powerful organic fertilizer made from cow dung, urine, jaggery, and pulse flour. It enhances microbial activity in the soil.
Neem oil spray (5ml per liter of water) is effective against sucking pests like aphids and jassids in most vegetable crops.
Drip irrigation can save up to 40-70% water compared to flood irrigation and reduces the incidence of weeds and diseases.
Currently, Basmati Rice, Organic Cotton, Sugarcane, and high-value vegetables like Broccoli and Red Bell Peppers are in high demand in both domestic and export markets.

Alluvial soil is the most fertile soil in India. It is ideal for growing Wheat, Rice, Sugarcane, Pulses, and Oilseeds. It requires consistent irrigation but minimal chemical supplements if maintained well.
Always use certified H.Y.V (High Yielding Variety) seeds. Testing seed germination (at least 70% success) before sowing can save you from 20-30% loss in total yield.
Yellow Sticky Traps are a great low-cost way to monitor and control whiteflies and aphids without using harmful chemicals.
